+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1ah","title":"fcp.postgresql: PostgreSQL Database Connector","description":"# fcp.postgresql - PostgreSQL Database Connector

## Overview
PostgreSQL database connector for SQL queries, transactions, and connection pooling.

## Archetype
**Database**

## Key Operations

### Query Operations
- `pg.query` - Execute parameterized SQL query
- `pg.execute` - Execute non-returning statement
- `pg.batch` - Execute multiple statements
- `pg.prepared` - Execute prepared statement
- `pg.explain` - Explain query plan

### Transaction Operations
- `pg.transaction.begin` - Start transaction
- `pg.transaction.commit` - Commit transaction
- `pg.transaction.rollback` - Rollback transaction
- `pg.transaction.savepoint` - Create savepoint
- Isolation levels: READ COMMITTED, REPEATABLE READ, SERIALIZABLE

### Schema Operations
- `pg.schema.tables` - List tables
- `pg.schema.columns` - Get column info
- `pg.schema.indexes` - List indexes
- `pg.schema.constraints` - Get constraints

### Advanced Features
- COPY protocol for bulk load/unload
- LISTEN/NOTIFY for event handling
- Row streaming for large results
- JSON/JSONB operations
- Full-text search support
- PostGIS spatial queries

### Connection Pool
- Configurable pool size
- Connection health checks
- Statement timeout
- Query logging

## Authentication
- Password authentication
- SSL/TLS connections
- Certificate authentication
- AWS IAM (RDS)
- Cloud SQL IAM (GCP)

## Providers Supported
- PostgreSQL (self-hosted)
- AWS RDS/Aurora
- Google Cloud SQL
- Azure Database
- Supabase
- Neon
- CockroachDB (compatible)

## Zone Architecture
\`\`\`
Zone 0 (Public):     Schema metadata
Zone 1 (Authorized): Read queries
Zone 2 (Private):    Write operations
Zone 3 (Sensitive):  DDL, admin operations
\`\`\`

## Security
- Parameterized queries (no SQL injection)
- Query whitelisting option
- Statement timeout enforcement
- Connection limit enforcement

## Dependencies
- fcp-core, fcp-config, fcp-testkit, fcp-telemetry","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:49:01.662979528-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:48:14.194007059-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1ah","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:16.288228342-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1ah","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:16.316226754-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1ah","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-5y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:16.343249881-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1j7","title":"fcp-security: Security Testing and Audit Framework","description":"# fcp-security: Security Testing and Audit Framework

## Overview
Security-focused library providing capability validation, input sanitization, audit logging, and security testing utilities for FCP connectors.

## Capability Token Validation
- Ed25519 signature verification
- Token expiration validation
- Issuer/audience verification
- Capability scope enforcement
- Resource constraint checking
- Constant-time comparison

## Zone Security
- Zone boundary enforcement
- Trust level validation
- Cross-zone access prevention
- Zone hierarchy traversal
- Provenance tracking

## Input Validation
- SQL injection detection
- XSS pattern detection
- Command injection patterns
- Path traversal detection
- SSRF prevention helpers
- Content-Type validation

## Audit Logging
- Security event capture
- Structured audit records
- Tamper-evident logging
- Correlation ID tracking
- PII redaction
- Retention policy support

## Security Testing
- Fuzzing helpers
- Property-based testing utilities
- Security assertion macros
- Vulnerability pattern matchers

## Cryptographic Utilities
- Secure random generation
- Key derivation functions
- Constant-time operations
- Secure memory handling (zeroize)

## Dependencies
- ed25519-dalek, sha2, hmac
- zeroize, rand
- regex (for pattern detection)

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-h1wi","title":"TEST: Connector Performance Benchmark Suite","description":"# TEST: Connector Performance Benchmark Suite

## Overview
Performance benchmarks ensuring connectors meet FCP performance targets from AGENTS.md.

## Performance Targets (from spec)
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Cold start | < 50ms |
| Request latency overhead | < 1ms |
| Memory footprint | < 10MB idle |
| Binary size | < 20MB (compressed) |

## Benchmark Categories

### Startup Benchmarks
- [ ] Cold start time measurement
- [ ] Warm start time
- [ ] Initialization overhead
- [ ] First request latency

### Latency Benchmarks
- [ ] Protocol overhead (JSON-RPC parsing)
- [ ] Capability validation overhead
- [ ] IPC round-trip time
- [ ] Introspection response time

### Memory Benchmarks
- [ ] Idle memory footprint
- [ ] Memory under load
- [ ] Memory leak detection (long-running)
- [ ] GC/allocation patterns

### Throughput Benchmarks
- [ ] Requests per second (single connector)
- [ ] Concurrent request handling
- [ ] Streaming throughput
- [ ] Event processing rate

### Resource Benchmarks
- [ ] CPU utilization at idle
- [ ] CPU under load
- [ ] File descriptor usage
- [ ] Network connection pooling

## Tooling
- criterion.rs for statistical benchmarks
- Custom harness for startup timing
- Memory profiling with jemalloc/heaptrack
- CI integration for regression detection

## Output
- JSON benchmark results
- Historical comparison
- Regression alerts (>10% degradation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:13:06.779412276-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:21:36.285666797-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-h1wi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:47:50.114423235-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-h32","title":"STANDARD: Connector Testing Requirements and Quality Gates","description":"# STANDARD: Connector Testing Requirements and Quality Gates

## Overview
Defines mandatory testing requirements and quality gates that ALL FCP connectors must pass before release.

## Unit Test Requirements

### Coverage Thresholds
- Overall: >80% line coverage
- Critical paths: >95% coverage
- Security code: >98% coverage

### Required Test Categories
- [ ] All FcpConnector trait methods
- [ ] Configuration validation (valid/invalid)
- [ ] Handshake success/failure scenarios
- [ ] Capability token validation
- [ ] Each operation (success, common errors, edge cases)
- [ ] Rate limit handling
- [ ] Timeout behavior
- [ ] Error recovery

### Mocking Requirements
- NEVER make real API calls in unit tests
- Use wiremock or similar for HTTP mocking
- Mock external service responses
- Test error injection (network, auth, rate limit)

## Integration Test Requirements
- [ ] Real handshake flow (with test credentials)
- [ ] Basic operation execution
- [ ] Event subscription lifecycle
- [ ] Reconnection handling

## E2E Test Requirements
- [ ] Cold start performance (<50ms)
- [ ] Protocol compliance verification
- [ ] Security boundary enforcement
- [ ] Resource usage within limits

## Quality Gates (CI/CD)
1. `cargo check --all-targets` - No errors
2. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - No warnings
3. `cargo fmt --check` - Properly formatted
4. `cargo test` - All tests pass
5. `cargo test --release` - Release tests pass
6. Coverage threshold met
7. `ubs` scan clean
8. No security vulnerabilities

## Documentation Requirements
- [ ] All public APIs documented
- [ ] AI hints for each operation
- [ ] Configuration schema documented
- [ ] Error codes documented","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:38:48.045099693-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:23:22.579821039-05:00"}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-h32","title":"STANDARD: Connector Testing Requirements and Quality Gates","description":"# STANDARD: Connector Testing Requirements and Quality Gates

## Overview
Defines mandatory testing requirements and quality gates that ALL FCP connectors must pass before release.

## Unit Test Requirements

### Coverage Thresholds
- Overall: >80% line coverage
- Critical paths: >95% coverage
- Security code: >98% coverage

### Required Test Categories
- [ ] All FcpConnector trait methods
- [ ] Configuration validation (valid/invalid)
- [ ] Handshake success/failure scenarios
- [ ] Capability token validation
- [ ] Each operation (success, common errors, edge cases)
- [ ] Rate limit handling
- [ ] Timeout behavior
- [ ] Error recovery

### Mocking Requirements
- NEVER make real API calls in unit tests
- Use wiremock or similar for HTTP mocking
- Mock external service responses
- Test error injection (network, auth, rate limit)

## Integration Test Requirements
- [ ] Real handshake flow (with test credentials)
- [ ] Basic operation execution
- [ ] Event subscription lifecycle
- [ ] Reconnection handling

## E2E Test Requirements
- [ ] Cold start performance (<50ms)
- [ ] Protocol compliance verification
- [ ] Security boundary enforcement
- [ ] Resource usage within limits

## Quality Gates (CI/CD)
1. `cargo check --all-targets` - No errors
2. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - No warnings
3. `cargo fmt --check` - Properly formatted
4. `cargo test` - All tests pass
5. `cargo test --release` - Release tests pass
6. Coverage threshold met
7. `ubs` scan clean
8. No security vulnerabilities

## CRITICAL: Detailed Logging Requirements

### All Tests MUST Include Comprehensive Logging

#### Log Levels by Test Phase
- **Setup**: INFO - test initialization, mock configuration
- **Execution**: DEBUG - every significant action
- **Assertions**: INFO - expected vs actual values
- **Teardown**: DEBUG - cleanup operations
- **Errors**: ERROR with full context and stack trace

#### Required Log Fields (Structured JSON)
Every log entry MUST include:
- `timestamp` - ISO 8601 with milliseconds
- `test_name` - Full test path (module::test_fn)
- `phase` - setup|execute|assert|teardown
- `correlation_id` - Unique per test run
- `duration_ms` - Time since test start
- `operation` - Current operation being tested

#### Operation-Specific Logging
- **HTTP Requests**: method, url, headers (redacted), body size, status code, latency_ms
- **WebSocket**: frame type, payload size, direction (send/recv)
- **Events**: event type, payload hash, subscriber count
- **Errors**: error code, message, recoverable flag, retry_count

#### Log Format Example
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-11T12:00:00.123Z",
  "test_name": "discord::gateway::test_heartbeat_mechanism",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "a1b2c3d4",
  "duration_ms": 45,
  "operation": "send_heartbeat",
  "level": "DEBUG",
  "message": "Sending heartbeat frame",
  "details": {
    "sequence": 42,
    "interval_ms": 41250,
    "last_ack_at": "2026-01-11T11:59:58.500Z"
  }
}
```

#### Test Output Capture
- All tests run with `--nocapture` in CI for full visibility
- Failed tests include complete log history in reports
- Logs retained for 30 days for debugging
- Performance metrics extracted from logs for trending

#### Assertions MUST Log:
- What is being asserted
- Expected value
- Actual value
- Context (why this assertion matters)

```rust
tracing::info\!(
    expected = %expected_code,
    actual = %response.status(),
    context = "Gateway should accept valid heartbeat",
    "Asserting heartbeat response status"
);
assert_eq\!(response.status(), expected_code);
```

#### Error Scenario Logging
When testing error conditions:
- Log the injected error condition
- Log the expected error handling behavior
- Log actual error propagation
- Log recovery actions if applicable

## Documentation Requirements
- [ ] All public APIs documented
- [ ] AI hints for each operation
- [ ] Configuration schema documented
- [ ] Error codes documented","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:38:48.045099693-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:48:40.157176324-05:00"}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hc2","title":"fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner","description":"# Scheduled Task Runner Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Time-based automation for FCP zones.

## Overview

Many automation workflows require scheduled execution: daily reports, periodic syncs, cleanup jobs, reminder systems. This meta-connector provides a robust scheduling infrastructure that triggers FCP actions on time-based schedules.

## Core Components

### 1. Cron Expression Scheduling
- Standard cron syntax (5-field and 6-field)
- Extended syntax with seconds precision
- Timezone-aware scheduling
- Named schedules for common patterns
- Cron expression validation and preview

```
# Standard patterns
@hourly    = 0 * * * *
@daily     = 0 0 * * *
@weekly    = 0 0 * * 0
@monthly   = 0 0 1 * *

# Custom patterns
*/15 * * * *     = Every 15 minutes
0 9 * * 1-5      = 9 AM on weekdays
0 0 1,15 * *     = 1st and 15th of month
```

### 2. One-Time Scheduled Tasks
- Schedule task for specific datetime
- Support for relative scheduling ("in 30 minutes")
- Cancellation before execution
- Rescheduling capability
- Batch scheduling (multiple one-time tasks)

### 3. Interval-Based Triggers
- Fixed interval execution (every N minutes/hours/days)
- Sliding window intervals
- Jitter support for load distribution
- Minimum interval enforcement
- Pause/resume capability

### 4. Task History and Logs
- Complete execution history
- Stdout/stderr capture
- Execution duration tracking
- Success/failure status
- Searchable log storage
- Log retention policies

### 5. Failure Alerting
- Configurable alert channels (email, Slack, PagerDuty)
- Alert on:
  - Task failure
  - Task timeout
  - Consecutive failures
  - Missed schedules
- Alert suppression/snooze
- Escalation policies

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[schedules]]
name = "daily-backup"
cron = "0 2 * * *"
timezone = "America/New_York"
action = "zone.backup.run_backup"
params = { full = true }
timeout = "1h"
alert_on_failure = true

[[schedules]]
name = "hourly-sync"
interval = "1h"
jitter = "5m"
action = "zone.sync.pull_updates"
retry_on_failure = 3

[[one_time]]
name = "migration-task"
run_at = "2024-02-01T03:00:00Z"
action = "zone.db.run_migration"
params = { version = "v2.0" }
```

## Task State Machine

```
PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED
              ↓
           FAILED → RETRYING → COMPLETED/FAILED
              ↓
         DEAD (max retries exceeded)
```

## Dashboard Features

- Calendar view of scheduled tasks
- Next N upcoming executions
- Recent execution history
- Live execution monitoring
- Manual trigger capability
- Bulk operations (pause all, resume all)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Sub-second scheduling precision
- [ ] Support 1000+ concurrent schedules
- [ ] Missed task detection and alerting
- [ ] Distributed execution (no single point of failure)
- [ ] Complete audit trail
- [ ] Hot-reload of schedule configurations

## Dependencies

- Persistent storage for schedules and history
- Time synchronization (NTP)
- Alert channel integrations
- Zone action invocation system

## Considerations

- Handle daylight saving time transitions
- Cluster-safe (only one instance executes)
- Graceful shutdown (complete running tasks)
- Clock skew tolerance
- Long-running task support","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:23:20.104073834-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:35.225906099-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:35.225906099-05:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-hiu (fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner Connector)"}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hiu","title":"fcp.cron: Scheduled Task Runner Connector","description":"Time-based automation connector. Cron expression scheduling, one-time scheduled tasks, interval-based triggers, task history and logs, failure alerting. Enables agents to schedule recurring tasks like daily email summaries, weekly reports, nightly security scans.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:27:30.639093697-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:08:27.235633713-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T12:08:27.235633713-05:00","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: fcp.cron already completed and closed as flywheel_connectors-hc2."}

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","title":"fcp-ratelimit: Shared Rate Limiting Library","description":"# fcp-ratelimit: Shared Rate Limiting Library

## Overview
Production-grade rate limiting library supporting multiple algorithms, provider-specific header parsing, and intelligent backoff strategies.

## Algorithms

### Token Bucket
- Configurable bucket size and refill rate
- Burst handling
- Per-endpoint buckets

### Sliding Window
- Fixed window with sliding calculation
- Accurate request counting
- Memory-efficient implementation

### Leaky Bucket
- Smooth request pacing
- Queue management
- Overflow handling

## Provider Header Parsing
- X-RateLimit-Limit/Remaining/Reset (standard)
- Retry-After header
- X-Rate-Limit-* variations
- Provider-specific formats (GitHub, Twitter, Stripe, OpenAI)

## Backoff Strategies
- Exponential backoff with configurable base/max
- Decorrelated jitter
- Truncated exponential
- Custom strategy support

## Features
- Thread-safe implementations
- Async-first design
- Request queueing with priorities
- Quota tracking (daily/hourly/monthly)
- Multi-tenant support

## Integration
- Middleware for HTTP clients
- Decorator for FCP operations
- Metrics emission
- Logging integration

## Dependencies
- tokio (async), parking_lot (sync primitives)
- fcp-core, fcp-telemetry","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:36:25.276288662-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:22:03.71300347-05:00"}
